hypothesis: Applying pulsed galvanostatic operation (10 Hz, 50% duty cycle) with peak current density equal to the champion steady-state value (≈249 mA cm−2) and the same time-averaged current will increase reactor yield by ≥20% relative to steady-state operation under identical electrolyte and flow conditions by improving i-CO2 mass transport and bubble detachment, thereby simultaneously raising JCO and UCO2.
